description: V0.2.0 Developer Documentation Rewrite — replace all outdated docs with three fresh, tested, developer-first docs written from source code

userStories.title: Delete outdated docs
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want to remove all outdated documentation so that new docs start from a clean slate.
acceptanceCriteria: All files in docs/ are deleted (including subdirectories: deployment/, filters/, images/, learning-manual/)
docs/ directory exists but is empty (ready for new content)
No broken internal links remain in CLAUDE.md or other project files referencing deleted docs
userStories.notes: docs/quickstart.md was already deleted before this PRD started. Verified no stale references.

userStories.title: Write getting-started.md — Prerequisites through Boot
userStories.description: As a new developer, I want clear setup instructions so that I can get Flowplane running from scratch.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/getting-started.md exists with sections: Prerequisites, Install, Boot the stack, Verify
Prerequisites lists Docker and Rust with version requirements (verified against source)
Install command (git clone + cargo install --path . --locked) is tested and output captured
Boot command (flowplane init --with-envoy --with-httpbin) is tested and output captured
Verify steps (flowplane status, health check curl) are tested and output captured
Port range verified against source code (expected 10001-10020)
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-04-03. Port range 10001-10020 confirmed from docker-compose-envoy.yml.

userStories.title: Write getting-started.md — Expose and Test
userStories.description: As a new developer, I want to expose httpbin through the gateway and test it so that I see Flowplane working end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: Expose section appended to docs/getting-started.md after the Verify section
Uses CLI only — no MCP column or MCP examples
flowplane expose command tested against the live dev stack, real output captured
Curl test through the gateway (port 10001-10020) works and output captured
Read src/cli/expose.rs and src/domain/ to document accurate behavior and flags
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-04-03. expose source on release/v0.2.0 branch (src/cli/expose.rs). Docker stack must be built from release/v0.2.0 for expose API to work.

userStories.title: Write getting-started.md — Add a Rate Limit Filter
userStories.description: As a new developer, I want to add rate limiting to my exposed API so that I understand Flowplane's filter system.
acceptanceCriteria: Rate limit section appended to docs/getting-started.md after Expose section
Filter create command uses correct nested config structure ({type, config}) verified against source (src/domain/filter.rs)
Filter create and filter attach commands tested against live stack, real output captured
Rate limiting verification tested — show 200/200/200/429/429 curl pattern with real output
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-04-03. Filter type is local_rate_limit (external rate_limit not implemented). JSON uses camelCase filterType and nested config.type + config.config structure.

userStories.title: Write getting-started.md — Explore, Tear Down, and Prod Mode
userStories.description: As a new developer, I want to know what else I can do and how to clean up, plus a brief intro to production mode.
acceptanceCriteria: Explore section appended to docs/getting-started.md: list, status, doctor, swagger-ui, MCP connection info
Tear down command (flowplane down) tested and output captured
Prod mode section is brief: make up ENVOY=1 HTTPBIN=1, seed, auth login, dev/prod comparison table
Prod mode clearly marked as untestable without Zitadel (if applicable)
Next steps section links to cli-reference.md, filters.md
Explore and tear down commands tested against live stack
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-04-03. Explore commands (status, doctor, list, swagger-ui, MCP endpoints) tested against live stack. Tear down (down, down --volumes) tested. Prod mode section documents make up ENVOY=1, seed, auth login, and dev/prod comparison table.

userStories.title: Write cli-reference.md — Stack Management and Global Flags
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a CLI reference for stack management commands so that I can operate Flowplane confidently.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/cli-reference.md created with global flags section at the top
Stack management commands documented: init, down, serve, status, doctor, list
Each command's flags and arguments verified by reading src/cli/ source files
Every example command tested against the live stack with real output captured
after_long_help output included for key commands if present in source
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-04-03. Stack management commands (init, down, serve, status, doctor, list, logs, database) documented with flags from source and real output from live stack.

userStories.title: Write cli-reference.md — Expose, Unexpose, and Resource CRUD
userStories.description: As a developer, I want accurate reference docs for resource management commands.
acceptanceCriteria: Expose/unexpose section appended to docs/cli-reference.md with 409 conflict behavior and validation errors
Resource CRUD documented: cluster, listener, route-config, filter (create, list, show, update, delete)
Filter attach/detach documented with examples
Every JSON payload example tested against the live stack with real output captured
Input validation rules noted: valid names, positive ports, valid upstream URLs
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Write cli-reference.md — Import OpenAPI and Learning Sessions
userStories.description: As a developer, I want reference docs for import and learning session commands.
acceptanceCriteria: Import OpenAPI section appended to docs/cli-reference.md with flags and examples
Learning sessions section documented: start, stop, status, list
Every example tested against the live stack with real output captured
Read src/cli/ source files for accurate flags and arguments
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Write cli-reference.md — Secrets, Auth, and Config
userStories.description: As a developer, I want reference docs for secrets, auth, and config CLI commands.
acceptanceCriteria: Secrets section appended to docs/cli-reference.md — secret create --config uses correct syntax (no redundant type field)
Auth commands documented: login, logout, whoami, token — note that whoami shows email/name in prod mode
Config commands documented: show, path, set
Every example tested against the live stack with real output captured
Read src/cli/ source for accurate flags
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Write filters.md — Overview, Types Table, and Config Structure
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to understand how filters work and see all available types at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/filters.md created with overview section: create -> attach -> verify workflow
Filter types table lists ALL filter types read from src/domain/filter.rs (not from memory)
Config structure section explains nested {type, config} format with example
Per-route overrides documented with example
Detach and delete workflow documented with tested commands
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Write filters.md — Traffic filters (rate_limit, router, cors, compressor)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want working examples for traffic management filters.
acceptanceCriteria: One section per filter appended to docs/filters.md: rate_limit, router, cors, compressor
Each filter has a working JSON create example tested against the live stack
Real output captured for each create and attach command
Source code for each filter type read before writing the example
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Write filters.md — Auth and security filters (jwt_authn, ext_authz, rbac, credential_injector)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want working examples for auth and security filters.
acceptanceCriteria: One section per filter appended to docs/filters.md: jwt_authn, ext_authz, rbac, credential_injector
credential_injector example includes secret creation (SDS) and full test flow
Each filter has a working JSON create example tested against the live stack
Real output captured for each create and attach command
Source code for each filter type read before writing the example
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Write filters.md — Remaining filters (header_mutation, health_check, lua, tap, ext_proc, wasm, gzip, bandwidth_limit)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want working examples for all remaining filter types.
acceptanceCriteria: One section per remaining filter type appended to docs/filters.md
Verify the complete filter list from src/domain/filter.rs — include any filters not covered by US-011/US-012
Each filter has a working JSON create example tested against the live stack
Real output captured for each create and attach command
Source code for each filter type read before writing the example
No guessed or fabricated output
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Check src/domain/filter.rs for the actual list — the filter names in this story title are approximate. Include whatever is actually in the codebase.

userStories.title: Correct skills that contradict source code
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want skills to match reality so that future AI assistance is accurate.
acceptanceCriteria: Review all contradictions discovered during doc writing (check progress.txt notes)
Update any skill files (.claude/skills/) where content contradicts source code
Log each correction with the file path and what was wrong vs what is correct
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is a sweep at the end — contradictions should be noted in progress.txt as they are discovered during earlier stories.

userStories.title: Final end-to-end verification walkthrough
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want confidence that the docs work as a complete sequence for a new user.
acceptanceCriteria: Tear down existing stack: flowplane down --volumes
Follow getting-started.md from step 1 as a brand new user
Every command runs successfully in sequence — no step fails
Continue through cli-reference.md testing every example
Continue through filters.md testing every example
Any failures found are fixed in the docs
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Second pass — catches ordering issues and state dependencies between examples.

userStories.title: Create Excalidraw diagrams (stretch)
userStories.description: As a new developer, I want visual diagrams showing the developer workflow and architecture.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/developer-workflow.excalidraw created — 6-step developer journey cycle with dashed green annotation arrow
docs/architecture.excalidraw created — left-to-right component diagram (Developer -> Control Plane -> Envoy -> Upstream)
Hand-drawn style (roughness: 1), color scheme per prompt spec
Diagrams referenced in getting-started.md with markdown image syntax or excalidraw.com note
Created using Excalidraw MCP tools (read_me then create_view)
userStories.notes: STRETCH — docs are complete without this. Only attempt if all other stories pass.
